key_insight: SciPhyRL turns portfolio optimization into a physics-informed continuous-time control problem, learning cost-aware trading policies directly from historical trajectories via a pathwise Hamilton-Jacobi formulation.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a data-driven way to deploy multi-period allocation policies that explicitly balance return, risk, and transaction costs, making it relevant for scalable decision systems under real market frictions.
method: Reformulates portfolio control in an extended state space with cumulative costs
Solves the HJB/pathwise Hamilton-Jacobi equation offline using PINN from observed trajectories
Uses discrete target holdings and a quadratic price-impact model for practical execution
evidence_signal: On a 14-asset ETF universe with an engineered oracle signal, the learned Gibbs policy showed strong out-of-sample Sharpe ratio gains versus static and myopic baselines.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and stability of the engineered signal and on how well the quadratic impact model matches real execution conditions.
paper_title: SciPhy Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Optimization
